ticket_title: BE - Add MDN to device line items on receipt

test_cases.name: TC-1: MDN renders on device line item for single-line new activation [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Subscriber created; IDV outcome = Validated; one Handset selected; one rate plan attached. | Test Data: deviceType = Handset, storeType = COR, role = Store Rep, mdnLifecycle = new (e.g., 7022345678), inventoryType = D (ASTOCK)

steps.action: Complete activation through the Payment step.
Trigger receipt generation in both digital and print form.
Open the receipt and locate the device line item.
steps.expected: The device line item displays the activated MDN beside or beneath the device model and IMEI.

test_cases.name: TC-2: Multi-line activation — each device line shows its own MDN [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Cart contains 2+ devices, each provisioned with distinct MDNs in the same transaction. | Test Data: device1 = Handset (MDN-A), device2 = Wearable paired to a different MDN (MDN-B), storeType = COR, role = Store Rep

steps.action: Add two devices to the cart and complete activation for both.

steps.expected: Each device line shows its correct MDN with no cross-line bleed; values match what the activation API returned.

test_cases.name: TC-3: Port-in MDN renders correctly on receipt [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Activation flow with port-in path completed; port-in MDN reserved. | Test Data: mdnLifecycle = port-in, sourceCarrier = another prepaid MVNO, deviceType = Handset

steps.action: Verify the MDN value on the device line item.
steps.expected: Receipt shows the ported MDN exactly as it was reserved (not a temporary aging MDN).

test_cases.name: TC-4: Receipt without device (accessory-only purchase) does not regress [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Cart contains only accessory line items; no activation occurred. | Test Data: lineItems = [Case, Charger], no IMEI, no MDN

steps.expected: Receipt renders accessory lines normally with no empty `MDN:` label and no null/undefined string in the rendered output.

test_cases.name: TC-5: BYOD / BYOP activation — MDN appears on the bring-your-own device line [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Customer brings own device; IMEI passes TAC / blacklist check; SIM swapped or eSIM provisioned. | Test Data: sourcing = BYOD, simType = eSIM, deviceType = Handset

steps.expected: The MDN renders on the BYOD device line in the same format as for in-stock ASTOCK devices.

test_cases.name: TC-6: CPNI-aware display — MDN is masked or full-shown per Metro policy [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: CPNI display rule confirmed with product / legal (full vs last-4). | Test Data: role = Store Rep, role = CSR, role = Customer (compare receipt surfaces if they differ)

steps.action: Generate a receipt for the Store Rep at a COR store.
Generate a CSR remote view of the same transaction.
Compare the MDN display format on each surface.
steps.expected: MDN renders per the documented CPNI display rule on each surface and the audit log records that MDN was displayed.

test_cases.name: TC-7: Reprint of an old receipt fetches MDN from the persisted line item [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: An activation receipt printed 24h+ ago is reprinted from EzPOS history. | Test Data: historical transaction id; deviceLine.persistedMDN snapshot

steps.action: Open a past activation in the receipt history.

steps.expected: The reprinted receipt shows the MDN that was active at the time of sale (read from the persisted line item) and not a live HLR lookup that could drift if the MDN was later changed.
